claim: Independent cross-check confirms: YOLO demand router correctly implements evidence scope discrimination with four categories (project, external, user, unknown); project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths; external-only evidence is blocked from satisfying existing-project claims; boundary audit prevents mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines exactly four scope categories: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: Core schema definition matches explorer claim.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles; all have writes_code=false and result_schema=yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1.
evidence.why: Agent protocols confirm read-only evidence gathering contract.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope infers scope from explicit declaration, path, URL, or source field; maps project_code/project_test/project_docs/project_config/project_log/project_artifact to 'project' scope.
evidence.why: Scope inference logic validates evidence classification rules.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope checks if record explicitly declares a valid scope from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES.
evidence.why: Validation ensures evidence records declare required scope field.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator checks for repo-relative path field; returns false for URLs or missing paths.
evidence.why: Project evidence must have concrete file locators, not URLs.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when project facts lack project-scoped evidence; returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED when project scope lacks path.
evidence.why: Readiness enforcement prevents unverified project claims.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates file hash map excluding allowed write roots; diffBoundarySnapshots detects added/modified/deleted files.
evidence.why: Boundary audit detects any project file mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt hard-codes agent rules: boundary forbids target project modification; every evidence record must include scope; project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths; external evidence is background only.
evidence.why: Agent prompt enforces evidence scope discipline at contract level.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution; diff results in BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers; prevents agents from modifying target project files.
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement prevents mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Test proves external-only evidence (url=https://example.com, scope=external) triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker for existing-project facts.
evidence.why: Test validates external evidence cannot prove project state.

evidence.summary: Test proves project evidence without path triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker; confirms project evidence requires repo-relative path.
evidence.why: Test validates project evidence must have file locators.

evidence.summary: Doctrine specifies three evidence agent protocols: explorer (read-only), cross-checker (independent verification), verifier (evidence adequacy check); conflicts must become blockers not averages.
evidence.why: Documentation codifies cross-check philosophy and conflict handling.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative paths; external evidence is background only and cannot prove existing project state.
evidence.why: Documentation codifies evidence scope philosophy and contract.

risks: Evidence agents could still misclassify scope if path patterns overlap (e.g., a URL-like string in a path field).
Boundary snapshot uses file hash which could miss mutations if files change and change back within the same agent run.
Scope inference logic has many heuristics that could misclassify edge cases.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all explorer claims. Evidence scope discrimination is correctly implemented across schema, router, dispatch, tests, and docs. Project facts require project-scoped evidence with paths; external-only evidence is blocked; boundary audit prevents mutations. No conflicts found.
